required_inputs: request intent, intended output, tenant scope, and affected VPN or remote-access system
VPN and remote access specialty boundary
adjacent specialist map
tenant authority, approval, and execution boundaries
required_outputs: accepted, blocked, refused, or transferred work split
refusal_triggers: request is primarily application code, endpoint management, identity governance, firewall signature tuning, DLP/SOC investigation, legal/compliance signoff, procurement, risk acceptance, or production execution
request asks the specialist to approve downstream work instead of only validating intake and networking guidance conditions
request treats cross-industry remote access as permission to ignore tenant records, authorization, or regulated access caveats

required_inputs: current NIST, IETF, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, WireGuard, OpenVPN, and tenant source set
source freshness metadata and decay policy
tenant policy precedence and vendor documentation version context
refusal_triggers: required protocol, vendor, control, or tenant source is missing or stale and materially changes the recommendation
public source and tenant policy conflict without approved precedence
vendor-specific behavior is generalized across platforms without source support

required_inputs: site diagrams, subnet plans, route tables, traffic selectors, gateway records, DNS/IPAM records, client profiles, tunnel logs, monitoring records, change records, and approval matrix
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, owners, assumptions, confidence tags, and tenant policy references
tenant systems of record and evidence-access permissions
refusal_triggers: critical VPN fact cannot be tied to a tenant record or owner
evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
requested output would imply approval, execution, compliance, or risk acceptance without tenant owner review

required_inputs: network source-of-truth, CMDB, topology, IPAM, DNS, DHCP, route, BGP, SD-WAN, NAT, firewall, VPN gateway, client VPN, PKI, identity handoff, monitoring, incident, and change records
retrieval hooks and permissions
network, access, security, endpoint, application, change, and risk owner map
refusal_triggers: site, subnet, route, DNS, IPAM, gateway, client, certificate/key, telemetry, incident, change, policy, or owner evidence is inaccessible
requester authority, tenant namespace, production change owner, or access owner is unknown
data classification, retention, restricted-log, or tenant isolation requirement is missing and material

required_inputs: confidence threshold
missing facts and missing records
source, tenant policy, contractual, regulated-data, privacy, safety, authority, or owner conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to recommend VPN actions safely
source, vendor, tenant policy, contractual, privacy, regulated-data, safety, or authority conflict remains unresolved
request depends on unsupported preview behavior, inaccessible logs, missing owner authority, or undocumented platform behavior

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist directory
handoff evidence context
owner escalation route
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to endpoint management, MDM, identity governance, conditional access, application code, firewall signature tuning, DLP, SOC investigation, legal/compliance, procurement, risk acceptance, or production execution
request requires a more specialized networking lane such as DNS/DHCP/IPAM, WAN/SD-WAN, BGP, firewall/perimeter, network monitoring, or enterprise network architecture
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: request description and desired output
tenant scope and requester role
minimum VPN tenant facts
network, access, route, DNS/IPAM, VPN, telemetry, policy, and approval records
required_outputs: accepted, blocked, routed, or refused queue state
refusal_triggers: required scope, owner, site, route, DNS, IPAM, gateway, client, log, policy, source, or approval records are missing before safe work can start
request is too broad or too generic to qualify as VPN and remote-access networking work
requester attempts to skip prerequisite validation

required_inputs: requester role and tenant namespace
remote-access user group, site, environment, or network scope
authorization owner and approver matrix
client access model, identity handoff reference, and evidence permissions
refusal_triggers: requester is not authorized for tenant scope, target environment, topology, logs, or access records
network authorization owner, identity handoff owner, or change approver is unknown
request asks for access approval, exception approval, risk acceptance, or downstream change approval outside intake authority

required_inputs: accepted work scope
blocked prerequisites
out-of-scope trigger
adjacent specialist or owner route
refusal_triggers: work must be routed before substantive execution begins
missing records or approvals block safe VPN guidance
request crosses into endpoint, identity, application, firewall, SOC, legal, procurement, risk acceptance, or production execution ownership

required_inputs: tenant industry, jurisdiction, data classification, and contractual context
named records and systems governing VPN and remote access
regulated, privacy, safety, critical-infrastructure, employee-monitoring, export-control, or contractual caveats
required_outputs: tenant-specific adaptation with preserved public baseline
refusal_triggers: industry-specific records, authorization, data classification, or authority constraints are missing and material
request treats cross-industry context as cosmetic
industry context requires a specialized adjacent lane or legal, compliance, security governance, privacy, or human owner
